Q005 Medium Find the email about passkeys and tell me the deadline for when Microsoft-provided SMS authentication will be retired. 📌 The email titled "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" states that Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. ⚡ The email regarding passkeys was sent by **microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com** on **July 22, 2026**. ### **Retirement Details** * **Email Subject:** Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication * **Retirement Deadline:** February 1, 2027 ### **Key Milestones Mentioned:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users enabled for SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and nudged to register one during their next MFA. * **February 1, 2027:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication are fully retired in Microsoft Entra ID. After this date, users relying solely on these methods will receive a blocking prompt to register a passkey before they can sign in. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): The email titled "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" states that Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. Q013 Basic Draft a short reply to the Microsoft Entra security email acknowledging the passkey update. 📌 I have drafted a reply to the email "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" acknowledging the update. ⚡ Subject: RE: Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication Dear Microsoft Entra Security Team, This email serves as an acknowledgment of the upcoming security updates regarding the transition to passkeys and the retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication. I have noted the following key milestones for our tenant: * **September 1, 2026:** Automatic enablement of passkeys for users on SMS/voice. * **February 1, 2027:** Full retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication. Delete the recipient's address and retype it manually. If an Auto-Complete suggestion appears, do not select it. 2. **Verify the address with the recipient:** Contact the recipient through another method (such as by phone) to confirm that the email address exists and is spelled correctly. 3. **Check for incorrect email forwarding:** Ask the recipient to verify if they have set up any email forwarding rules that might be directing messages to an incorrect or non-existent address. Security: Transition to Phishing-Resistant Authentication** Microsoft sent a high-priority notification regarding the retirement of legacy multi-factor authentication (MFA) methods. * **Issue:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication are being retired in favor of passkeys to combat phishing, SIM-swap, and replay attacks. * **Critical Deadlines:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users on SMS/voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register them. * **February 1, 2027:** SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired. * **Action Required:** Jesus must identify users still relying on SMS/voice, initiate a passkey registration campaign, and update internal documentation before the auto-enablement date to avoid user login blocks. ### **2. The recipient address was not found. * **Impact:** Critical Q4 planning materials have not reached the intended stakeholder. * **Action Required:** Jesus needs to verify if Sarah Jenkins’ email address is misspelled, check if she has a valid Office 365 license, or confirm if her account has been correctly synchronized in the directory. ### **Summary of Communications (July 22, 2026)** | Time | Type | Subject | Key Concern | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | 11:05 AM | Inbox | Passkeys by default... | Security migration from SMS/Voice MFA | | 12:48 PM | Inbox | Undeliverable: Q4 Resource Allocation | Failed delivery to Sarah Jenkins | 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): Jesus faces two issues from July 22: 1. Q050 Complex Summarize the 'More Info for Email Admins' section from the delivery failure email received yesterday. 📌 The section advises admins to: 1. Confirm the recipient address exists, 2. Ensure directory synchronization is correct for hybrid environments, 3. Check for errant forwarding rules, 4. Verify the recipient has a valid Office 365 license, and 5. Check MX records and mail flow connectors. ⚡ Based on the delivery failure email received on July 22, 2026, regarding the message to **sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com**, here is a summary of the **'More Info for Email Admins'** section: ### **Error Overview** * **Status Code:** 550 5.1.10 * **Error Detail:** `RESOLVER.ADR.RecipientNotFound` * **Cause:** The recipient address is incorrect or does not exist at the destination domain. While the recipient's server reports the error, it typically requires a fix from the sender's side. ### **Recommended Admin Actions** If the standard "How to Fix It" steps fail, admins should investigate the following: * **Address Verification:** Confirm that the recipient's email address exists, is spelled correctly, and is capable of accepting messages. * **Directory Synchronization:** In hybrid environments, ensure the recipient's address is synchronized correctly between the on-premises directory and Office 365. * **Forwarding Rules:** Check for any errant mail flow rules or inbox forwarding settings that might be redirecting the mail to an invalid address. * **Licensing:** Verify that the recipient has an active Office 365 license assigned in the Admin Center. * **Mail Flow & MX Records:** Ensure that domain MX records are configured correctly with the registrar and that Office 365 mail flow connectors are set up properly. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): The section advises admins to: 1. Confirm the recipient address exists, 2. Ensure directory synchronization is correct for hybrid environments, 3. Check for errant forwarding rules, 4. Verify the recipient has a valid Office 365 license, and 5. Check MX records and mail flow connectors. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 100.0% 100.0% ⚡ 7.93s ⏱️ 1.98s

Q057 Medium Search my inbox for any emails related to "passkeys" or authentication changes. 📌 There is one email titled 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication', received July 22, 2026 at 11:05 AM UTC, and it is currently unread. ⚡ I found one email in your inbox related to "passkeys" and authentication changes: ### **Email Details** * **Subject:** Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication * **From:** microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com * **Received:** 2026-07-22T11:05:49Z ### **Key Takeaways** Microsoft is transitioning to phishing-resistant authentication. Here are the critical details and dates mentioned in the email: * **Default Change:** Passkeys are becoming the default authentication experience in Microsoft Entra ID. * **Key Deadlines:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users currently using SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register one during their next MFA. * **February 1, 2027:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired. After this date, users who have not migrated will receive a blocking prompt to register a passkey before they can sign in. * **Recommended Actions:** 1. **Identify affected users** still relying on SMS or voice. 2. **Enable passkeys** and run a registration campaign before the September 1 deadline. 3. **Communicate the change** to your organization. 4. **Evaluate third-party providers** if you have a regulatory requirement to maintain SMS/voice (options available starting September 18, 2026). 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): There is one email titled 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication', received July 22, 2026 at 11:05 AM UTC, and it is currently unread. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 33.3% 100.0% ⚡ 5.98s ⏱️ 1.62s

This typically happens if the address is misspelled or the user does not exist in the directory. ### **Recommended Actions** According to the notification, you can attempt the following: 1. **Check Spelling:** Verify the recipient's email address and retype it manually (do not use the Auto-Complete suggestion). 2. **Send Again:** In Outlook on the web, select the NDR and click the link "To send this message again, click here." 3. **Directory Sync:** Since you are the admin, ensure the user is correctly synced in Office 365 and has a valid license assigned. 4. **Contact Recipient:** Verify via another method (like phone) that the email address is correct and active. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): Found: 'Undeliverable: Q4 Resource Allocation' received July 22, 2026, stating the message to sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com couldn't be delivered because the recipient wasn't found at sockcop.onmicrosoft.com. Q059 Complex Based on the body content of the Passkeys email, when will Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication retire? 📌 According to the email body, Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. ⚡ Based on the email titled **"Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication"** received on July 22, 2026, the retirement details are as follows: * **Retirement Date:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired on **February 1, 2027**. ### Key Timeline & Impact: * **September 1, 2026:** Users enabled for SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register one during their next MFA completion. * **February 1, 2027:** Full retirement. After this date, users whose only MFA method is SMS or voice will receive a blocking prompt requiring them to register a passkey before they can sign in. * **Exceptions:** Customer-managed telecom providers configured through the Microsoft Security Store are not affected by this retirement. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): According to the email body, Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 100.0% 100.0% ⚡ 4.57s ⏱️ 1.53s
Q060 Complex Looking across both messages in my inbox, which one is unread and what security-related change does it describe? 📌 The unread message is 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication' (received July 22, 2026). It describes Microsoft Entra ID's move to passkeys as the default authentication method, with SMS and voice authentication retiring February 1, 2027. ⚡ Based on the tenant data, the unread message and the security-related change it describes are detailed below: ### **Unread Message** * **Subject:** Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication * **Sender:** microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com * **Received:** 2026-07-22T11:05:49Z * **Status Reasoning:** This message is part of a pair of emails received on July 22nd. While the other message from that day (an "Undeliverable" NDR) was likely read and acted upon—evidenced by the follow-up "Q4 Resource Allocation" email sent on July 23rd—this policy notification remains the unread security update. ### **Security-Related Change Description** The email outlines a significant transition in Microsoft Entra ID authentication security aimed at moving toward **phishing-resistant** protection. * **Passkeys as the Default:** Passkeys are becoming the default authentication experience for all Microsoft Entra ID tenants to mitigate vulnerabilities associated with SIM-swapping, replay attacks, and phishing. * **Retirement of SMS and Voice:** Microsoft-provided telecom delivery for SMS and voice authentication is being retired. * **Key Deadlines:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users currently using SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and "nudged" to register a passkey during their next MFA completion. * **February 1, 2027:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired. Users who have not transitioned will encounter a blocking prompt to register a passkey before they can sign in. * **Impact:** Organizations must move users to phishing-resistant methods (ideally passkeys) before the February 2027 deadline to avoid service disruption. (Note: Customer-managed telecom providers are unaffected.) 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): The unread message is 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication' (received July 22, 2026). It describes Microsoft Entra ID's move to passkeys as the default authentication method, with SMS and voice authentication retiring February 1, 2027. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 100.0% 100.0% ⚡ 46.27s ⏱️ 1.81s

Q071 Basic Please reply to the Passkeys authentication email acknowledging receipt of the notice. 📌 A reply would be sent to the 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication' email (received July 22, 2026) acknowledging the notice about SMS/voice authentication retiring February 1, 2027. ⚡ To acknowledge the notice regarding Passkeys authentication, a reply has been prepared for the relevant email found in your inbox. ### **Reply Details** * **To:** microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com * **Subject:** RE: Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication * **Message:** > Hello, > > This email serves as an acknowledgment of receipt regarding the notice for "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" scheduled for enforcement starting September 1, 2026, and full retirement by February 1, 2027. > > We have noted the required actions to identify affected users and transition them to phishing-resistant authentication methods. > > Best regards, > Jesus Chavez > admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com *** **Note:** The original notice was sent from a "no-reply" address (`microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com`). While the reply has been drafted as requested, automated senders typically do not monitor incoming messages. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): A reply would be sent to the 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication' email (received July 22, 2026) acknowledging the notice about SMS/voice authentication retiring February 1, 2027. Here is your executive briefing for today, **July 27, 2026**, based on your Microsoft 365 data. ### **📅 Calendar: Today's Schedule** You have one meeting scheduled for today: * **Executive Committee Weekly Alignment** * **Time:** 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM (15:00 – 16:00) * **Organizer:** Jesus Chavez --- ### **📩 Inbox Status: Action Required** You have several unread messages requiring attention, prioritized by urgency: #### **High Priority & Alerts** * **Delivery Failure: Q4 Resource Allocation** * **From:** Microsoft Exchange * **Issue:** Your email to `sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com` was not delivered because the recipient was not found. You may need to verify the spelling or check the directory for Sarah's correct address. * **AzureAD App Credentials Nearing Expiration** * **From:** dtt_sc_azautoapp@deloitte.com * **Issue:** The client secret for App ID `7868d053-cf9c-4848-be5a-f9bbf8279234` is expiring soon. Action is required to prevent service disruption. * **Security Update: Passkeys & SMS Authentication Retirement** * **From:** Microsoft Security * **Issue:** Microsoft is moving to passkeys. SMS and voice authentication will be retired by February 2027. You are encouraged to begin migrating users to phishing-resistant methods before September 1, 2026. #### **Operational Tasks** * **Athena: GIDs Travel & MDM Sync Deployment** * **From:** dsharma47@deloitte.com * **Task:** The sync job deployment is ready; please review the employee sync logs. * **Retired APMs List** * **From:** Lisa Bendorf * **Task:** Review the attached spreadsheet of Application Performance Monitoring tools scheduled for decommission. --- ### **ℹ️ For Your Information (FYI)** * **Cloud Cost Optimization:** Summary received from `cloudcost@deloitte.com` regarding Azure subscription best practices. * **Weekly Digest:** BrightTALK digest regarding upcoming presentations on cybersecurity and AI enterprise workflows. **Summary:** Your morning is clear for focused work or addressing the Azure credential and delivery failure issues before your 3:00 PM alignment meeting. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): As of today, July 23, 2026, there is 1 unread email: 'Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication' (received July 22, 2026), notifying about SMS/voice authentication retiring February 1, 2027. There are no calendar meetings scheduled for today. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 50.0% 100.0% ⚡ 8.67s ⏱️ 1.69s
Q074 Complex Summarize the key action items from my inbox: the undeliverable email and the passkeys notice, and recommend next steps. 📌 1) 'Undeliverable: Q4 Resource Allocation' (July 22, 2026): the email to sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com bounced with error 550 5.1.10 because the recipient wasn't found - action: verify the correct email address and resend. 2) 'Passkeys by default...' (July 22, 2026, unread): Microsoft-provided SMS/voice authentication retires February 1, 2027 - action: migrate to passkey-based authentication before that date. ⚡ Based on your inbox, here is a summary of the key action items and recommended next steps regarding the undeliverable email and the Microsoft passkeys notice. ### 1. Undeliverable Email: Q4 Resource Allocation An email you sent to **sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com** was returned with a **550 5.1.10 error (RecipientNotFound)**. This means the message could not be delivered because the email address does not exist in your tenant. **Key Action Items:** * **Verify Recipient:** Confirm if "sarah.jenkins" is the correct alias. Since you are the admin, check the **Active Users** list in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center to see if her account is listed under a different name or domain. * **Resend Spreadsheet:** Once the address is corrected, resend the "Q4 Resource Allocation" spreadsheet to ensure she receives the data for review. **Recommended Next Steps:** 1. **Manual Entry:** When resending, do **not** use the Auto-Complete suggestion in Outlook. Manually type the address to ensure no cached errors are being reused. 2. **Check Licensing:** If the user exists but the email still fails, verify that Sarah has an active Office 365 license assigned. --- ### 2. Passkeys and SMS/Voice Retirement Notice Microsoft is transitioning all Entra ID tenants to phishing-resistant authentication. **Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be retired on February 1, 2027.** **Key Action Items & Deadlines:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users currently using SMS/voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register one. * **February 1, 2027:** Full retirement of SMS and voice. Users without a passkey or other MFA will face a blocking prompt. **Recommended Next Steps:** 1. **Identify Impacted Users:** Run a report in the Entra ID portal to identify which users still rely on SMS or voice for Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). 2. **Enable Passkeys Policy:** Update your Authentication Methods Policy to enable and encourage the use of passkeys (FIDO2) before the September auto-enablement date. 3. **Communication Campaign:** Send an internal announcement to staff explaining the transition to passkeys and the security benefits (protection against phishing and SIM-swapping). --- ### Summary Table of Action Items | Category | Issue | Priority | Next Step | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **Email Delivery** | Sarah Jenkins (Recipient Not Found) | **High** | Verify her email in Admin Center and resend Q4 spreadsheet. | | **Security** | SMS/Voice Auth Retirement | **Medium** | Audit user MFA methods and draft a passkey migration plan. | | **Security** | AzureAD App Credentials | **Medium** | Renew client secret for App ID `7868d053...` (referenced in your inbox). | 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): 1) 'Undeliverable: Q4 Resource Allocation' (July 22, 2026): the email to sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com bounced with error 550 5.1.10 because the recipient wasn't found - action: verify the correct email address and resend. 2) 'Passkeys by default...' (July 22, 2026, unread): Microsoft-provided SMS/voice authentication retires February 1, 2027 - action: migrate to passkey-based authentication before that date. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 100.0% 100.0% ⚡ 10.9s ⏱️ 1.94s

Key highlights include: * **Key Dates:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users on SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and nudged to register one. * **February 1, 2027:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired. * **Required Action:** Identify users currently using SMS or voice and migrate them to passkeys or other phishing-resistant methods before the February 2027 deadline to avoid blocking login prompts. * **Exceptions:** Customer-managed telecom providers configured through the Microsoft Security Store are not affected by this retirement. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): Found one email: "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication", received July 22, 2026 at 11:05 AM UTC, currently unread. Q083 Complex Based on the body content of the passkeys email, when will Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication retire? 📌 According to the email body, Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. ⚡ Based on the email titled "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication," the retirement schedule is as follows: * **Retirement Date:** Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will fully retire on **February 1, 2027**. ### Key Timeline & Impact: * **September 1, 2026:** Users enabled for SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register one during their next MFA completion. * **February 1, 2027:** Microsoft-provided telecom delivery for SMS and voice is decommissioned. Users whose only MFA method is SMS or voice will encounter a blocking prompt to register a passkey before they can sign in. * **Exceptions:** Customer-managed telecom providers configured through the Microsoft Security Store are not affected by this retirement. 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): According to the email body, Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will retire on February 1, 2027. Q096 Basic Reply to the passkeys retirement email acknowledging that I've reviewed the SMS/voice authentication retirement notice. 📌 A reply would be sent on the "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" email thread, acknowledging review of the February 1, 2027 retirement date. ⚡ Based on your mailbox data, here is the draft reply to the notification from Microsoft regarding the authentication changes. ### **Draft Email Reply** **To:** microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com **Subject:** RE: Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication Dear Microsoft Entra ID Team, I am writing to acknowledge that I have received and reviewed the notice regarding the retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication, as well as the transition to passkeys as the default authentication method. I have noted the critical deadlines for our tenant (**sockcop.onmicrosoft.com**): * **September 1, 2026:** Users enabled for SMS/voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and prompted to register. * **February 1, 2027:** Full retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication. We will proceed with the recommended steps to identify affected users and initiate the migration to phishing-resistant passkeys to ensure no service disruption after the enforcement date. Best regards, **Jesus Chavez** Admin | sockcop.onmicrosoft.com 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): A reply would be sent on the "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" email thread, acknowledging review of the February 1, 2027 retirement date. [Source: Federated Microsoft 365 Search Broadcast] 100.0% 100.0% ⚡ 37.29s ⏱️ 2.49s

Q100 Complex Synthesize an action plan for Jesus Chavez based on today's mailbox state: what needs a resend, what needs a read, and what security notice needs attention before its deadline. 📌 Jesus Chavez should: 1) Resend the bounced "Q4 Resource Allocation" email to a corrected sarah.jenkins address, 2) Read the unread "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" email, and 3) Take action on the SMS/voice authentication retirement before the February 1, 2027 deadline. ⚡ Based on the mailbox state for **Jesus Chavez**, here is the synthesized action plan: ### 1. Items Requiring Resend * **Q4 Resource Allocation:** The message sent to `sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com` on July 23, 2026, was returned as **undeliverable** (Error 550 5.1.10). * **Action:** Verify Sarah Jenkins' correct email address (the NDR indicates the recipient was not found), update the recipient field, and resend the Q4 resource allocation spreadsheet. ### 2. Items Requiring Review (Needs a Read) * **Athena Employee Data Sync Logs:** An email from `dsharma47@deloitte.com` indicates the GIDs Travel & MDM sync job deployment is ready. You need to review the **employee sync logs**. * **Retired APMs List:** Lisa Bendorf provided a spreadsheet of all **Application Performance Monitoring tools** scheduled for decommission. This should be reviewed to ensure no critical dependencies remain. * **Cloud Cost Optimization:** Review the "Operational Excellence on Cloud Cost" summary from `cloudcost@deloitte.com` regarding Azure subscription practices. * **App Credential Expiry:** An alert from `dtt_sc_azautoapp@deloitte.com` warns that the **client secret for App ID 7868d053-cf9c-4848-be5a-f9bbf8279234** is nearing expiration and requires administrative action. ### 3. Critical Security Notice & Deadlines **Notice:** Retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and Voice Authentication. * **Description:** Microsoft Entra ID is moving to phishing-resistant authentication (Passkeys). SMS and voice-based MFA will be retired. * **Upcoming Deadlines:** * **September 1, 2026:** Users enabled for SMS/voice will be automatically nudged to register passkeys. You should move users manually before this date to maintain control over the schedule. * **February 1, 2027:** Full retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication. * **Immediate Action Required:** Identify users in the `sockcop.onmicrosoft.com` tenant currently relying on SMS or voice and begin a migration campaign to Passkeys or other phishing-resistant methods. ### Summary Table for Jesus Chavez | Priority | Category | Topic | Key Detail/Deadline | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **High** | **Resend** | Q4 Resource Allocation | Correct `sarah.jenkins` address (Error 550) | | **High** | **Security** | App Credential Expiry | Update secret for App ID 7868d053 | | **Medium** | **Action** | Athena Sync Job | Review employee sync logs | | **Medium** | **Security** | Auth Method Migration | **Sept 1, 2026** (Auto-enablement begins) | | **Low** | **Read** | Retired APMs List | Review decommissioning schedule | 🌐 According to your connected Microsoft 365 Outlook Assistant (admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com): Jesus Chavez should: 1) Resend the bounced "Q4 Resource Allocation" email to a corrected sarah.jenkins address, 2) Read the unread "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" email, and 3) Take action on the SMS/voice authentication retirement before the February 1, 2027 deadline. 🔬 Multi-Intent Gap: Why Gemini MCP Scored 50% on Q097

In Q097, the user query asked for "Executive briefing on inbox alerts and calendar schedule".

• Gemini MCP (50% Score): Invoked only tool_list_meetings and returned the 2 Teams meetings, but omitted the inbox alerts (Passkeys & Azure Copilot).
• StreamAssist (100% Score): Federated Search simultaneously queried both the email and calendar connectors, retrieving both.
